descriptions.description: This replication package contains code for reproducing the results in “Pre-test with Caution: Event-study Estimates After Testing for Parallel Trends”, by Jonathan Roth (AER: Insights). This is a partial replication package, see http://doi.org/10.3886/E151982V1 for the full replication package, including the original analyses e-used here! This package does not contain the code or data to re-run the replications of the original analyses.

descriptions.description:
----- Overview -----Thank you for coming this appendix.This page provides a replication package to you for our each research question.
- (RQ1) To what degree are reviews connected?- (RQ2) Why are reviews being connected?- (RQ3) To what degree can connection types be automatically recovered?
----- Description -----
[package_rq1] This directory includes (i) scripts to crawl original review datasets via Gerrit Code Review system, (ii) scripts to generate a review graph for each software community, (iii) all review graphs of OpenStack, AOSP, Qt, Eclipse, and Libreoffice.
[package_rq2] This directory includes (i) scripts to obtain our sample datasets for manual classification and (ii) our coded samples of Nova and Neutron projects

[package_rq3] This directory includes (i) scripts to perform feature extraction, bootstrap sampling, hyperparameter optimization, model construction, and performance evaluation, and (ii) the data of results for automated classification in Nova and Neutron projects.

descriptions.description:
This repository contains all required data, tools, scripts, and complete documentation for replication.
README provides all necessay details about each directory under this artifact. Additionally, we have provided README for individual RQ.
The structure of the artifact is as folows: - evosuite-artifacts -- contains the original test suite generated by EvoSuite for all 25 subjects - RQ1 -- replication package for RQ1, includes data, scripts and documentation - RQ2 -- replication package for RQ2, includes data, scripts and documentation - RQ3 -- replication package for RQ3, includes data, scripts and documentation - README --- documentation for entire package

descriptions.description:
The replication package contains all data files and programs (Stata) in order to reproduce the tables and figures of the manuscript. 

Please refer to v4 (DOI 10.5281/zenodo.16924821) for the finalised replication package which generates identical results, removing certain personally identifying information that had inadvertently been left in the original data (v1).

titles.title: Replication package for: Disability and risk preferences: Experimental and survey evidence from Vietnam

descriptions.description:
This replication package accompanies the paper“CAPRA: Scaling Feedback on Software Architecture Deliverables with a Multi-Agent LLM System”.

Specifically, the package includes:

• A fully self-contained Java-based re-implementation of the CAPRA pipeline built on the Spring AI framework, together with the dependent Python microservices used for document parsing and multi-modal extraction• The CSV export of the Software Engineering (SWE) features extracted via the SALLMA-based workflow and clustered using HDBSCAN for automated Knowledge Base construction• The complete set of 10 CAPRA-generated PDF feedback reports (Teams 06, 12–20)• The full annotation dataset containing binary ratings assigned by two independent evaluators across 8 assessment criteria• One original student architectural deliverable (shared with explicit consent)

The package enables full replication of the experimental evaluation described in the paper, including inter-rater agreement computation and Knowledge Base generation.

All thresholds, architectural decisions, and pipeline stages described in the paper are preserved.

titles.title: CAPRA Replication Package (Artifact)

descriptions.description: This dataset contains all the few-shot models trained on five folds (both on data with and without pre-processing) on the Dronology Dataset. These models are additional material to the replication package available Here. Please cite the original paper (information available here: https://github.com/a66as/REFSQ2023-ReqORNot/ ).
Additional material (2/2) for the replication package: https://github.com/a66as/REFSQ2023-ReqORNot/

descriptions.description: This is the replication package for "Task Mismatch and Salary Penalties: Evidence from the Biomedical PhD Labor Market," accepted in 2024 by the Journal of Labor Economics.

This is a ZIP archive containing the full replication package, including all code, spreadsheets, and figures, with original folder structure preserved.
To access its contents, rename the file extension to .zip after downloading, then extract normally.

descriptions.description: Replication Package for "Cross-National Differences in Market Response: Line-Length, Price, and Distribution Elasticities in Fourteen Indo-Pacific Rim Economies" (available at https://doi.org/10.1177/00222437211058102). For more information about the replication package see also the following Github repository: https://github.com/hannesdatta/marketingmix-journal-of-marketing-research.

Monthly product sales data across fourteen product categories, at the national level (i.e., number of iPhones sold in Singapore in August 2012); covering 2004-2014. Full description see paper. The replication package contains code and file descriptions, but not the original data (given the NDA between the main data supplier, GfK, and the co-authors).

rightsList.rights: Custom terms specific to this dataset

titles.title: Replication Package for: Cross-National Differences in Market Response: Line-Length, Price, and Distribution Elasticities in Fourteen Indo-Pacific Rim Economies

descriptions.description:
This repository contains code, datasets, precomputed analysis artifacts, and experimental results for EMRefactor: Enhancing Extract Method Refactorings with Large Language Models and Heuristic Search.

Data

''data/'' contains the main method-level input data and metadata for EMRefactor, including the community corpus and the extended corpus.''ast/'' contains the precomputed AST structure files used for statement mapping and structural search.''data/ccg/'' contains the precomputed code context graph files used for dependency-aware refactoring generation.''repositories/'' contains the original repositories/case directories and oracle data used for evaluation.''sampled dataset/ci95_sample_321/'' contains the sampled dataset, project lists, oracle subset, and selection metadata used in the RQ2 and RQ3 experiments.''RQ1/'', ''RQ2/'', ''RQ3/'', and ''RQ4/'' contain the experimental outputs for the main evaluation, sensitivity analysis, ablation study, and quality-metric analysis.CodeThe ''scripts/'' folder contains the main source code for LLM prompting, response cleaning, AST-based mapping, dependency-based candidate expansion, search-based refactoring generation, and evaluation. The ''tool/GraphCoder/'' folder contains supporting utilities for graph-related analysis and artifact rebuilding. The package also includes corresponding documentation files, such as ''README.md'' and ''RUN_COMMANDS.md''.

titles.title: Replication Package for EMRefactor
